title: Inline Scanning, Delimiters, Escapes, Code Spans, and Emphasis · Retrieval Circuit
summary: A delayed publication review reconstructs inline scanning, delimiters, escapes, code spans, and emphasis from revision, configuration, source bytes, document nodes, route manifest, rendered HTML, asset graph, validation report, artifact digest, deployment, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for bounded inline tokenizer and delimiter resolver after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debuggi…
objectives: Advance one cursor through text, escapes, entities, code spans, delimiters, links, and literal fallbacks without dropping characters.
Match bounded backtick runs, normalize permitted interior whitespace, preserve code text, and reject missing closure predictably.
Classify delimiter runs from surrounding characters, track opener and closer state, and resolve nested emphasis deterministically.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

For Static-site publishing and Python Separate evidence from confidence · Review Inline Complexity, challenge the documented misconception with changed-source, delimiter, Unicode, nesting, route, URL, asset, viewport, cache, security, deployment, recovery, and controlled production-transfer evidence: Short normal posts prove inline parsing has acceptable complexity.

stimulus.title: Inline Complexity case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A review publishing team handles case 9243 during a compromised-content recovery rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to consume inline input exactly once while bounding delimiter work and preserving literals; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native filesystem, template engine, validator, network, GitHub, DNS, TLS, CDN, and host effects; then replace a trusted author fixture with untrusted raw HTML, unsafe URLs, and an active SVG. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Bound input length, delimiter work, nesting, diagnostics, and worst-case time with changed adversarial fixtures.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Short normal posts prove inline parsing has acceptable complexity.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Bound input length, delimiter work, nesting, diagnostics, and worst-case time with changed adversarial fixtures. Artifact under review: bounded inline tokenizer and delimiter resolver.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Inline Complexity, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Inline Complexity record says bounded inline tokenizer and delimiter resolver looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary for Inline Complexity.

For separate evidence from confidence, render one trusted short page at the root, omit content authority, source spans, parser limits, route base, URL and asset reconciliation, contextual encoding, accessibility, clean-build comparison, deployment, rollback, recovery, and production transfer, then accept plausible HTML as publication proof. This does not satisfy Inline Complexity for bounded inline tokenizer and delimiter resolver.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Bound input length, delimiter work, nesting, diagnostics, and worst-case time with changed adversarial fixtures.
